SKOV3 cell counts

Leander Meuris

2025-10-14

Data

The data are from a flow cytometry experiment which analyzed the number of CART cells in the blood or spleen of mice carrying a tumor and treated with different versions of CAR T-cells or controls. The following variables are in the dataset:

  • Experiment: from which experiment are the data (Exp 30, 32, 43)
  • Timepoint: from which moment in the experiment? We have “Early”, “Mid” and “Late”, corresponding to day 34, 80/88/85 (Exp 30, 32, 43) and 116/123/123 (Exp 30, 32 ,43).
  • Treatment: mice received no CAR T-cells (PBS, NTC, None), CAR T-cells but no MGAT5 engineering (CD70 nanoCAR-neg and CD70 nanoCAR-WT) or CAR T-cells which were MGAT5 KO (CD70 nanoCAR-MGAT5 KO)
  • CellCount: the number of cells found in a spleen or in 1 ul blood.
  • Location: whether spleen or blood was analyzed.
data <- read_xlsx(here('data/FACSdata_clean_new.xlsx'))

Make a group variable, indicating no CAR, CAR or CAR with MGAT5 KO. Then make 6 subsets of the data: Spleen and Blood, each early, mid, late.

data <- data %>%
  filter(!is.na(CellCount))

Early_TP <- c(34)
Mid_TP <- c(80, 88, 85)
End_TP <- c(116, 123)

data <- data %>%
  mutate(Experiment = paste0('Exp', Experiment),
         Day_Meas_Early = 34,
         Day_Meas_Mid = case_when(
           Experiment == 'Exp30' ~ 80,
           Experiment == 'Exp32' ~ 88,
           Experiment == 'Exp43' ~ 85),
         Day_Meas_End = case_when(
           Experiment == 'Exp30' ~ 116,
           Experiment %in% c('Exp32', 'Exp43') ~ 123),
         TimePoint = case_when(
           Day %in% Early_TP ~ 'Early',
           Day %in% Mid_TP ~ 'Mid',
           Day %in% End_TP ~ 'Late'),
         Group = factor(case_when(
           Treatment %in% c('PBS', 'NTC', 'None') ~ 'Control',
           Treatment %in% c('CD70 nanoCAR-neg', 'CD70 nanoCAR-WT') ~ 'CAR',
           Treatment %in% c('CD70 nanoCAR-MGAT5 KO','CD70 nanoCAR-MGAT5KO') ~ 'MGAT5KO_CAR'),
           levels = c('Control', 'CAR', 'MGAT5KO_CAR'),
           labels = c('WT T-cells or None', 'CD70 nanoCAR', 'CD70 nanoCAR - MGAT5 KO')),
         TimePoint = factor(TimePoint, 
                            levels = c('Early', 'Mid', 'Late'),
                            labels = c('Early', 'Mid', 'Endpoint')),
         ID = paste0(Experiment,'M', Mouse),
         Experiment = factor(Experiment,
                             levels = c('Exp30', 'Exp32', 'Exp43'),
                             labels = c('Experiment A', 'Experiment B', 'Experiment C')),
         Location = factor(Location),
         plCC = pseudo_log(CellCount))

We note that cell counts can be lower than 1 if we look in blood. For cell counts we would like to use a Poisson or Negative binomial model, however, these don’t work well with non-integers. So we will work with the number of cells per ml blood and round to the nearest integer.

For spleens, we don’t have counts below 1, so there we just round to the nearest integer.

data <- data %>%
  mutate(CellCount = ifelse(Location == 'Blood',
                            (1000*CellCount) %>% round(., 0),
                            CellCount %>% round(., 0)))

We also want to add the info on which mice are tumor free or have a tumor at the different moments of measurement.

Ctrl <- read_xlsx(here('data/Survival.xlsx')) %>%
  mutate(ID = paste0('Exp',Experiment,'M',Mouse),
         Experiment = factor(paste0('Exp', Experiment),
                             levels = c('Exp30', 'Exp32', 'Exp43'),
                             labels = c('Experiment A', 'Experiment B', 'Experiment C')))

data <- left_join(data, Ctrl)

We first determine the last day of any measurement for each mouse

data <- left_join(data, data %>%
                    group_by(ID) %>%
                    summarise(max_time = max(pmax(
                      Day, TimeFUP_FullCtrl, TimeFUP_PartialCtrl, 
                      TimeFUS_FullCtrl, TimeFUS_PartialCtrl, 
                      DayRelapse, Daylastmeasurement,na.rm = T), 
                      na.rm = T), .groups = 'drop'))

We define classes of outcome by the mid or end timepoint. - mouse dead before - tumor growing - tumor stasis/shrinking - tumor free

First for the mid timepoint

data <- data %>%
  mutate(Status_Mid = case_when(
    # Control mice and special case Exp A
    is.na(StatusPrimary) ~ NA,
    Experiment == 'Experiment A' &
      StatusPrimary == 'No control' ~ 'Tumor',
    Experiment == 'Experiment A' &
      StatusPrimary == 'Control, no relapse' ~ 'No tumor',
    # Mouse died before measurement
    Day_Meas_Mid > max_time &
      Experiment %in% c('Experiment B', 'Experiment C') ~ 'Dead',
    # Mouse never controlled tumor
    Day_Meas_Mid <= max_time &
      StatusPrimary == 'No control' ~ 'Tumor',
    # Mouse partially controlled tumor, then relapse
    Day_Meas_Mid <= max_time &
      StatusPrimary == 'Partial control, relapse' &
      Day_Meas_Mid < TimeFUP_PartialCtrl ~ 'Tumor',
    Day_Meas_Mid <= max_time &
      StatusPrimary == 'Partial control, relapse' &
      between(Day_Meas_Mid, TimeFUP_PartialCtrl, DayRelapse) ~ 'Tumor',
    Day_Meas_Mid <= max_time &
      StatusPrimary == 'Partial control, relapse' &
      DayRelapse < Day_Meas_Mid ~ 'Tumor',
    # Mouse fully controlled tumor, then relapse
    Day_Meas_Mid <= max_time &
      StatusPrimary == 'Control, relapse' &
      Day_Meas_Mid < TimeFUP_FullCtrl ~ 'Tumor',
    Day_Meas_Mid <= max_time &
      StatusPrimary == 'Control, relapse' &
      between(Day_Meas_Mid, TimeFUP_FullCtrl, DayRelapse) ~ 'No tumor',
    Day_Meas_Mid <= max_time &
      StatusPrimary == 'Control, relapse' &
      DayRelapse < Day_Meas_Mid ~ 'Tumor',
    # Mouse fully controlled tumor, never relapsed
    Day_Meas_Mid <= max_time &
      StatusPrimary == 'Control, no relapse' &
      Day_Meas_Mid < TimeFUP_PartialCtrl ~ 'Tumor',
    Day_Meas_Mid <= max_time &
      StatusPrimary == 'Control, no relapse' &
      Day_Meas_Mid >= TimeFUP_PartialCtrl ~ 'No tumor',
    ))

data <- data %>%
  mutate(Status_Mid = factor(Status_Mid, 
                             levels = c('No tumor', 'Tumor', 'Dead')))

Then for the end timepoint

data <- data %>%
  mutate(Status_End = case_when(
    Experiment == 'Experiment A' &
      max_time == 34 ~ 'No tumor',
    # Mouse died before measurement
    Day_Meas_End > max_time ~ 'Dead',
    # Exp A, all mice cleared the tumor
    Day_Meas_End <= max_time & 
      Experiment == 'Experiment A' ~ 'No tumor',
    # Mouse never or only partially controlled tumor
    Day_Meas_End <= max_time &
      StatusSecondary %in% c('No control', 'Partial control') ~ 'Tumor',
    # Mouse fully controlled the tumor
    Day_Meas_End <= max_time &
      StatusSecondary == 'Control' ~ 'No tumor',
    # Mouse never developed a tumor
    Day_Meas_End <= max_time &
      StatusSecondary == 'No tumor' ~ 'No tumor'
    ))

data <- data %>%
  mutate(Status_End = factor(Status_End, 
                             levels = c('No tumor', 'Tumor', 'Dead')))

Disease course

data <- data %>%
  mutate(T1_to_T3 = paste0('Tumor - ', Status_Mid, ' - ', Status_End),
         T1_to_T2 = paste0('Tumor - ', Status_Mid),
         T2_to_T3 = paste0(Status_Mid, ' - ', Status_End))

Exploratory data analysis

Plot the data by experiment and timepoint. First for blood data, then for spleen data. We also use a pseudo-log-scale y axis to better see differences. (A pseudo log-scale uses a log scale for large values but gradually becomes a linear scale for small values)

BloodBoxplots <- data %>%
  filter(Location == 'Blood') %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = Group, y = CellCount, color = Group)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA) +
  geom_jitter(alpha = 0.4, width = 0.2) +
  facet_grid(Experiment~TimePoint) +
  scale_y_continuous(trans = 'pseudo_log', 
                     breaks = c(0,5,25,150,1000,10000,100000)) +
  scale_x_discrete(guide = guide_axis(angle = 90)) +
  theme_bw() +
  scale_colour_manual(values = c(
    rgb(44,20,83, maxColorValue = 255), 
    rgb(14,111,124, maxColorValue = 255),
    rgb(251,79,6, maxColorValue = 255))) +
  labs(y = 'Cells per ml of blood')

SpleenBoxplots <- data %>%
  filter(Location == 'Spleen') %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = Group, y = CellCount, color = Group)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA) +
  geom_jitter(alpha = 0.4, width = 0.2) +
  facet_grid(Experiment~TimePoint) +
  scale_y_continuous(trans = 'pseudo_log', 
                     breaks = c(0,5,25,150,1000,10000,100000)) +
  scale_x_discrete(guide = guide_axis(angle = 90)) +
  theme_bw() +
  scale_colour_manual(values = c(
    rgb(44,20,83, maxColorValue = 255), 
    rgb(14,111,124, maxColorValue = 255),
    rgb(251,79,6, maxColorValue = 255))) +
  labs(y = 'Cells per Spleen')

#ggsave(here("output/BloodBoxplots.pdf"), BloodBoxplots,
#       device = "pdf", height = 8, width = 8, dpi=600)
#ggsave(here("output/SpleenBoxplots.pdf"), SpleenBoxplots,
#       device = "pdf", height = 8, width = 8, dpi=600)

BloodBoxplots

SpleenBoxplots

Models comparing MGAT5 vs WT per timepoint

Model

Summary: - Model separately per experiment and per timepoint - Don’t take into account which mice cleared the tumor

modeldata <- data %>%
  group_by(Location, TimePoint, Experiment) %>%
  nest()

Run models and output results of contrasts

# Running the models
modeldata <- modeldata %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(mod = list(glm(CellCount ~ Group, data = data, family = quasipoisson)),
         summ = list(summary(mod)), 
         Omnibus = list(car::Anova(mod, type = 2)),
         Coefnames = list(names(coef(mod))),
         ContMat = list(
           matrix((-1)*((str_detect(Coefnames, 'nanoCAR') %>% as.numeric) - 
                    (2*(str_detect(Coefnames, 'MGAT5') %>% as.numeric))), 
                               nrow = 1, 
                               ncol = length(Coefnames),
                               dimnames = list('MGAT5 vs nanoCAR WT',
                                               Coefnames))),
         data = list(data %>% mutate(
           pred = predict(mod, type = 'response'))),
         Table = list(left_join(
           multcomp::glht(mod, linfct = ContMat) %>% summary %>% tidy,
           multcomp::glht(mod, linfct = ContMat) %>% confint %>% tidy) %>%
             mutate(`Adj. p-value` = ifelse(
               adj.p.value < 0.001, "<0.001", round(adj.p.value, 3)) %>% as.character) %>%
             mutate(across(where(is.numeric)) %>% exp(.) %>% round(., 2)) %>%
             select(-null.value) %>%
             select(contrast, estimate,`95% CI, lower` = conf.low, `95% CI, upper` = conf.high,`Adj. p-value`)))

knitr::kable(modeldata %>%
               unnest(Table) %>%
               select(Experiment, TimePoint, contrast, estimate, `95% CI, lower`, `95% CI, upper`, `Adj. p-value`) %>%
               arrange(TimePoint, Experiment))
Location Experiment TimePoint contrast estimate 95% CI, lower 95% CI, upper Adj. p-value
Blood Experiment A Early MGAT5 vs nanoCAR WT 1.14 0.60 2.17 0.684
Spleen Experiment A Early MGAT5 vs nanoCAR WT 4.83 1.64 14.26 0.004
Blood Experiment B Early MGAT5 vs nanoCAR WT 7.21 3.23 16.13 <0.001
Blood Experiment C Early MGAT5 vs nanoCAR WT 11.95 1.14 125.56 0.039
Blood Experiment A Mid MGAT5 vs nanoCAR WT 3.66 0.31 42.69 0.301
Blood Experiment B Mid MGAT5 vs nanoCAR WT 1.24 0.61 2.52 0.549
Blood Experiment C Mid MGAT5 vs nanoCAR WT 6.41 0.51 81.13 0.151
Blood Experiment A Endpoint MGAT5 vs nanoCAR WT 4.50 0.90 22.55 0.067
Spleen Experiment A Endpoint MGAT5 vs nanoCAR WT 2.96 0.47 18.51 0.246
Blood Experiment B Endpoint MGAT5 vs nanoCAR WT 1.26 0.64 2.49 0.504
Spleen Experiment B Endpoint MGAT5 vs nanoCAR WT 0.90 0.39 2.07 0.811
Blood Experiment C Endpoint MGAT5 vs nanoCAR WT 5.22 0.60 45.41 0.135
Spleen Experiment C Endpoint MGAT5 vs nanoCAR WT 2.15 0.69 6.70 0.188

Data plots

modeldata$ID <- 1:nrow(modeldata)

data_aug <- bind_rows(modeldata$data, .id = "ID") %>%
  mutate(ID = as.numeric(ID))

data_aug <- left_join(data_aug, modeldata %>% select(ID, Experiment, Location, TimePoint))

BloodPlots <- data_aug %>%
  filter(Location == 'Blood') %>%
  filter(Group != 'WT T-cells or None') %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = Group, y = CellCount, color = Group)) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(y = pred, color = Group, ymin = pred, ymax = pred)) +
  geom_jitter(alpha = 0.4, width = 0.2) +
  facet_grid(Experiment~TimePoint) +
  scale_y_continuous(trans = 'pseudo_log', 
                     breaks = c(0,10,100,1000,10000,100000)) +
  scale_x_discrete(guide = guide_axis(angle = 90)) +
  theme_bw() +
  scale_colour_manual(values = c(
    #rgb(44,20,83, maxColorValue = 255), 
    rgb(14,111,124, maxColorValue = 255),
    rgb(251,79,6, maxColorValue = 255))) +
  labs(y = 'Cells per ml of blood')

BloodPlots

ggsave(here("output/BloodPlots.pdf"), BloodPlots,
       device = "pdf", height = 8, width = 8, dpi=600)
SpleenPlots <- data_aug %>%
  filter(Location == 'Spleen') %>%
  filter(Group != 'WT T-cells or None') %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = Group, y = CellCount, color = Group)) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(y = pred, color = Group, ymin = pred, ymax = pred)) +
  geom_jitter(alpha = 0.4, width = 0.2) +
  facet_grid(Experiment~TimePoint) +
  scale_y_continuous(trans = 'pseudo_log', 
                     breaks = c(0,10,100,1000,10000,100000)) +
  scale_x_discrete(guide = guide_axis(angle = 90)) +
  theme_bw() +
  scale_colour_manual(values = c(
    #rgb(44,20,83, maxColorValue = 255), 
    rgb(14,111,124, maxColorValue = 255),
    rgb(251,79,6, maxColorValue = 255))) +
  labs(y = 'Cells per ml of blood')

SpleenPlots

ggsave(here("output/SpleenPlots.pdf"), SpleenPlots,
       device = "pdf", height = 8, width = 6, dpi=600)

Plots are on the log scale, with observed data as dots and model predictions as lines. These lines are the predicted mean of each group. Plotted on the log scale, these tend to look ‘too high’, especially with right skew data, but are actually correct log transformed means. On the original scale they would look like correct means.

Methods

To analyse the FACS-based cell-count data of three separate experiments, we ran separate models for each combination of time-point (Early, Mid, Late/Endpoint), source (Blood or Spleen) and donor (= experiment). We don’t have data available for all combinations of factors: for Blood, data is lacking on non-engineered cells for the Mid timepoint in experiment A and C. For Spleen we don’t have any data for the Mid timepoint and we only have data from experiment A for the Early timepoint. Consequently, no models were run for these settings.

We ran quasipoisson models in R [ref], using the glm function with Cell Counts as the outcome variable and Group as predictor. An omnibus test for the main effects was performed using the Anova function (car package [ref]) with type 2 sums of squares. Next, we tested the specific contrast of MGAT5 engineered anti-CD70 nanoCAR T cells versus WT nanoCAR T cells. The contrast estimates, p-values and 95% confidence intervals were calculated using the multcomp package [ref].

Population contraction/expansion: TP1 -> TP2 and MGAT5 effect from one model

To answer this, we will look separately at the mice that have cleared the tumor vs. those that have not. We made a new variable for this (Status_Mid), based on the survival analysis. This variable captures whether a mouse has died, survived and carries a tumor at TimePoint 2 or survived and does not carry a tumor by timepoint 2.

A complication is that in experiment A the mice for which blood cell counts are available at the early time point were all sacrificed to count splenocytes. So in experiment A we do not have measurements on multiple time points for the same mice. The setup of experiment B and C was such that we do have multiple time points on the same mice (i.e. correlated data).

Model

Prepare data for modeling

modeldata <- data %>%
  filter(Location == 'Blood') %>%
  filter(TimePoint != 'Endpoint') %>%
  filter(Group != 'WT T-cells or None') %>%
  filter(Status_Mid != 'Dead') %>%
  droplevels

mdata <- modeldata %>%
  group_by(TimePoint, Status_Mid, Group, Experiment) %>%
  summarise(mCellCount = mean(CellCount, na.rm = TRUE))

modeldata <- left_join(modeldata, mdata)

Run model

mod <- glmmTMB(CellCount ~ TimePoint*Status_Mid*Group + Experiment*(TimePoint + Status_Mid + Group) + (1|ID),
              data = modeldata, family = nbinom2)

# glmmTMB::diagnose(mod)
# resids <- DHARMa::simulateResiduals(mod)
# plot(resids)

Model predictions

modeldata$pred <- predict(mod, type = 'response', re.form = NA)
  
T1_to_T2 <- modeldata %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = TimePoint, y = CellCount, color = Group)) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(y = pred, ymin = pred, ymax = pred)) +
  #geom_errorbar(aes(y = mCellCount, ymin = mCellCount, ymax = mCellCount, color = Group)) +
  geom_beeswarm(alpha = 0.6) +
  geom_line(aes(group = ID, color = Group)) +
  ggh4x::facet_nested(Experiment ~ T1_to_T2 + Group) +
  scale_y_continuous(trans = 'pseudo_log', breaks = c(0, 10, 100, 1000, 10000, 100000)) +
  scale_x_discrete(guide = guide_axis(angle = 90)) +
  theme_bw() +
  scale_colour_manual(values = c(
    #rgb(44,20,83, maxColorValue = 255), 
    rgb(14,111,124, maxColorValue = 255),
    rgb(251,79,6, maxColorValue = 255))) +
  labs(y = 'Cells per ml of blood')

ggsave(here("output/T1_to_T2.pdf"), T1_to_T2,
       device = "pdf",  height = 8, width = 10, dpi=600)

T1_to_T2

Plots are on the log scale, with observed data as dots and model predictions as lines. These lines are the predicted mean of each group. Plotted on the log scale, these tend to look ‘too high’, especially with right skew data, but are actually correct log transformed means. On the original scale they would look like correct means.

Contrasts

Output results of contrasts, first without multiple testing adjustment. This corresponds to what Nico asked (treat results of each experiment as fully independent). I would advise against.

emmA <- emmeans(mod, ~ TimePoint | Group*Status_Mid+Experiment, adjust = 'none',
                at = list(Experiment = 'Experiment A', Status_Mid = 'No tumor'))
ctrA <- pairs(emmA, type = 'response', reverse = TRUE, infer = TRUE, adjust = 'none')

emmB <- emmeans(mod, ~ TimePoint | Group*Status_Mid+Experiment, at = list(Experiment = 'Experiment B'))
ctrB <- pairs(emmB, type = 'response', reverse = TRUE, infer = TRUE, adjust = 'none')

emmC <- emmeans(mod, ~ TimePoint | Group*Status_Mid+Experiment, 
                at = list(Experiment = 'Experiment C', Status_Mid = 'Tumor'))
ctrC <- pairs(emmC, type = 'response', reverse = TRUE, infer = TRUE, adjust = 'none') 

table_nopadj <- summary(rbind(ctrA, ctrB, ctrC), adjust = 'none') 
table_nopadj <- table_nopadj %>% as_tibble %>%
  mutate(`Adj. p-value` = ifelse(p.value < 0.001, "<0.001", round(p.value, 3)) %>% as.character) %>%
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric)) %>% round(., 3)) %>%
  select(-null) %>%
  select(Experiment, Group, Status_Mid, 
         contrast,
         `Ratio of Cell Counts` = ratio,
         `95% CI, lower` = asymp.LCL, 
         `95% CI, upper` = asymp.UCL,
         `Adj. p-value`)

knitr::kable(table_nopadj)
Experiment Group Status_Mid contrast Ratio of Cell Counts 95% CI, lower 95% CI, upper Adj. p-value
Experiment A CD70 nanoCAR No tumor Mid / Early 0.141 0.031 0.637 0.011
Experiment A CD70 nanoCAR / MGAT5 KO No tumor Mid / Early 0.103 0.023 0.469 0.003
Experiment B CD70 nanoCAR No tumor Mid / Early 0.090 0.016 0.493 0.006
Experiment B CD70 nanoCAR / MGAT5 KO No tumor Mid / Early 0.066 0.014 0.310 <0.001
Experiment B CD70 nanoCAR Tumor Mid / Early 0.391 0.153 1.003 0.051
Experiment B CD70 nanoCAR / MGAT5 KO Tumor Mid / Early 0.394 0.133 1.163 0.092
Experiment C CD70 nanoCAR Tumor Mid / Early 1.205 0.407 3.562 0.736
Experiment C CD70 nanoCAR / MGAT5 KO Tumor Mid / Early 1.213 0.497 2.959 0.672

Next: same comparisons but with multiple testing adjustment. I recommend these, since all data are in the same model (with an adjustment for experiment).

table_padj <- summary(rbind(ctrA, ctrB, ctrC), adjust = 'mvt') 
table_padj <- table_padj %>% as_tibble %>%
  mutate(`Adj. p-value` = ifelse(p.value < 0.001, "<0.001", round(p.value, 3)) %>% as.character) %>%
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric)) %>% round(., 3)) %>%
  select(-null) %>%
  select(Experiment, Group, Status_Mid, 
         contrast,
         `Ratio of Cell Counts` = ratio,
         `95% CI, lower` = asymp.LCL, 
         `95% CI, upper` = asymp.UCL,
         `Adj. p-value`)

knitr::kable(table_padj)
Experiment Group Status_Mid contrast Ratio of Cell Counts 95% CI, lower 95% CI, upper Adj. p-value
Experiment A CD70 nanoCAR No tumor Mid / Early 0.141 0.017 1.135 0.079
Experiment A CD70 nanoCAR / MGAT5 KO No tumor Mid / Early 0.103 0.013 0.839 0.025
Experiment B CD70 nanoCAR No tumor Mid / Early 0.090 0.009 0.946 0.041
Experiment B CD70 nanoCAR / MGAT5 KO No tumor Mid / Early 0.066 0.008 0.561 0.005
Experiment B CD70 nanoCAR Tumor Mid / Early 0.391 0.106 1.438 0.31
Experiment B CD70 nanoCAR / MGAT5 KO Tumor Mid / Early 0.394 0.088 1.760 0.487
Experiment C CD70 nanoCAR Tumor Mid / Early 1.205 0.269 5.395 1
Experiment C CD70 nanoCAR / MGAT5 KO Tumor Mid / Early 1.213 0.353 4.165 0.999

Methods

To analyse the FACS-based cell-count data, we ran a generalized linear mixed model (GLMM) with a negative binomial error structure and a log link. We ran this count model in R [ref], using the glmmTMB function from the glmmTMB package [ref]. Cell Counts were the outcome variable, while Group (MGAT5 KO vs WT), outcome by the mid timepoint (tumor, no tumor), timepoint (early or mid) with all possible interactions and experiment (donor A, B or C) were the predictors. We added a random intercept for each mouse to model the correlation between the timepoints, as for donor B and C, mice were followed up over time. Summarising, the model formula was: CellCount ~ Group * Status_Mid * TimePoint + Experiment * (Group * Status_Mid * TimePoint) + (1|ID). Model quality was checked with the DHARMa package [ref]. Contrasts to test for cell population contraction by the mid timepoint and for the difference in CellCount in MGAT5 KO vs WT were calculated with the emmeans package [ref], multiplicity adjustment of the type ‘mvt’ (i.e. single step method).

Population contraction/expansion: TP2 -> TP3

To answer this, we will look separately at the mice that have cleared the secondary tumor vs. those that have not. We made a new variable for this (Status_End), based on the survival analysis. This variable captures whether a mouse has died, survived and carries a tumor at TimePoint 3 or survived and does not carry a tumor by timepoint 3.

In this case, we have paired measurements also for experiment A.

We present the data split up by disease course over the 3 timepoints.

modeldata <- data %>%
  filter(Location == 'Blood') %>%
  filter(TimePoint != 'Early') %>%
  filter(Group != 'WT T-cells or None') %>%
  filter(Status_Mid != 'Dead' & Status_End != 'Dead') %>%
  droplevels

mdata <- modeldata %>%
  group_by(TimePoint, Status_End, Group, Experiment) %>%
  summarise(mCellCount = mean(CellCount, na.rm = TRUE))

modeldata <- left_join(modeldata, mdata)
modeldata %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = TimePoint, y = CellCount, color = Group)) +
  #geom_errorbar(aes(y = mCellCount, ymin = mCellCount, ymax = mCellCount)) +
  geom_beeswarm(alpha = 0.6, aes(color = Group)) +
  geom_line(aes(group = ID)) +
  ggh4x::facet_nested(Experiment ~ T2_to_T3) +
  scale_y_continuous(trans = 'pseudo_log', breaks = c(0, 10, 100, 1000, 10000, 100000)) +
  scale_x_discrete(guide = guide_axis(angle = 90)) +
  theme_bw() +
  scale_colour_manual(values = c(
    #rgb(44,20,83, maxColorValue = 255), 
    rgb(14,111,124, maxColorValue = 255),
    rgb(251,79,6, maxColorValue = 255))) +
  labs(y = 'Cells per ml of blood')

Is it worthwhile analyzing this? There don’t seem to be clear trends as opposed to early to mid…

citation()

##   Grolemund G, Hayes A, Henry L, Hester J, Kuhn M, Pedersen TL, Miller
##   E, Bache SM, Müller K, Ooms J, Robinson D, Seidel DP, Spinu V,
##   Takahashi K, Vaughan D, Wilke C, Woo K, Yutani H (2019). "Welcome to
##   the tidyverse." _Journal of Open Source Software_, *4*(43), 1686.
##   doi:10.21105/joss.01686 <https://doi.org/10.21105/joss.01686>.
## 
## A BibTeX entry for LaTeX users is
## 
##   @Article{,
##     title = {Welcome to the {tidyverse}},
##     author = {Hadley Wickham and Mara Averick and Jennifer Bryan and Winston Chang and Lucy D'Agostino McGowan and Romain François and Garrett Grolemund and Alex Hayes and Lionel Henry and Jim Hester and Max Kuhn and Thomas Lin Pedersen and Evan Miller and Stephan Milton Bache and Kirill Müller and Jeroen Ooms and David Robinson and Dana Paige Seidel and Vitalie Spinu and Kohske Takahashi and Davis Vaughan and Claus Wilke and Kara Woo and Hiroaki Yutani},
##     year = {2019},
##     journal = {Journal of Open Source Software},
##     volume = {4},
##     number = {43},
##     pages = {1686},
##     doi = {10.21105/joss.01686},
##   }
citation('glmmTMB')
## To cite glmmTMB in publications use:
## 
##   Mollie E. Brooks, Kasper Kristensen, Koen J. van Benthem, Arni
##   Magnusson, Casper W. Berg, Anders Nielsen, Hans J. Skaug, Martin
##   Maechler and Benjamin M. Bolker (2017). glmmTMB Balances Speed and
##   Flexibility Among Packages for Zero-inflated Generalized Linear Mixed
##   Modeling. The R Journal, 9(2), 378-400. doi: 10.32614/RJ-2017-066.
## 
## To cite glmmTMB in publications use:
## 
##   McGillycuddy M, Warton DI, Popovic G, Bolker BM (2025).
##   "Parsimoniously Fitting Large Multivariate Random Effects in
##   glmmTMB." _Journal of Statistical Software_, *112*(1), 1-19.
##   doi:10.18637/jss.v112.i01 <https://doi.org/10.18637/jss.v112.i01>.
## 
## To see these entries in BibTeX format, use 'print(<citation>,
## bibtex=TRUE)', 'toBibtex(.)', or set
## 'options(citation.bibtex.max=999)'.
citation('DHARMa')
## To cite package 'DHARMa' in publications use:
## 
##   Hartig F (2024). _DHARMa: Residual Diagnostics for Hierarchical
##   (Multi-Level / Mixed) Regression Models_. R package version 0.4.7,
##   <https://CRAN.R-project.org/package=DHARMa>.
## 
## A BibTeX entry for LaTeX users is
## 
##   @Manual{,
##     title = {DHARMa: Residual Diagnostics for Hierarchical (Multi-Level / Mixed)
## Regression Models},
##     author = {Florian Hartig},
##     year = {2024},
##     note = {R package version 0.4.7},
##     url = {https://CRAN.R-project.org/package=DHARMa},
##   }
citation('emmeans')
## To cite package 'emmeans' in publications use:
## 
##   Lenth R (2025). _emmeans: Estimated Marginal Means, aka Least-Squares
##   Means_. R package version 1.11.1,
##   <https://CRAN.R-project.org/package=emmeans>.
## 
## A BibTeX entry for LaTeX users is
## 
##   @Manual{,
##     title = {emmeans: Estimated Marginal Means, aka Least-Squares Means},
##     author = {Russell V. Lenth},
##     year = {2025},
##     note = {R package version 1.11.1},
##     url = {https://CRAN.R-project.org/package=emmeans},
##   }
